sourceMovie.title: Hustlers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hustlers works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Hustlers later expanded. If Hustlers resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: The Old Man & the Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Hustlers — that funny edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into The Old Man & the Gun (2018). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Middle Men
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Middle Men (2009) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps Hustlers rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Zola
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Zola (2021) is the kind of film Hustlers fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The drama overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Big Short
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Big Short shares Hustlers's gift for drama — the 2015 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Carlito's Way
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Hustlers compelling — the drama, the crime — shows up in Carlito's Way (1993) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Magic Mike
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama energy of Hustlers is alive in Magic Mike (2012), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Anora
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Anora (2024) operates in the same drama as Hustlers, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Killing of a Chinese Bookie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Killing of a Chinese Bookie (1976) belongs to the same conversation as Hustlers — both anchor their drama in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Margin Call
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hustlers's drama stayed with you, Margin Call (2011) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hustlers?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, The Old Man & the Gun, Middle Men—each shares DNA with Hustlers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hustlers?
faq.answer: Hustlers (2019) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hustlers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
